text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little village, there lived a lonely old fox named Felix. Felix had golden fur, sharp eyes, and a reputation for being quite nosy. Despite being charming and witty, he rarely socialized. One foggy morning, however, he decided to venture outside for a brisk walk.

As he meandered through the village, past the orchard and the little stream, Felix spotted a group of birds nesting in a large oak tree. They were squaking loudly, sounding more distressed than cheerful, probably about the upcoming winter molt.

Felix couldn't stand it. He crept quietly closer, enjoying the rustling leaves. The birds continued their cacophony. With a soft thud, Felix decided he had enough.

With the quiet confidence that had earned him many secrets over the years, Felix carefully climbed up into the tree. The birds hopped aside, wary of this new predator.

Slowly, Felix reached down and gently plucked the oldest feather
